skill.name: performing-orthonotone-polychoral-instrument
skill.description: Guides agents through launching, playing, sculpting, and capturing performances with the Orthonotone polychoral instrument MVP. Use when generating music, soundscapes, or live demos from this repository.
skill.summary: Guides live performance setup, sound design, snapshots, MIDI control, and recording for the Orthonotone instrument.
skill.author: Domusgpt

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-severity patterns, but most are false positives from markdown links, documentation text, and words that contain weak-crypto substrings. Confirmed concerns are limited to community-sourced instructions that run npm/npx commands and advise screen/audio capture, so the skill is medium risk and publishable with warnings.

medium_findings.title: Community Skill Runs Local npm Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run npx http-server, npm install, and npm run check. These commands are plausible for a browser-based instrument, but they execute community project dependencies and scripts on the user's machine.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in the instructions and execute local package tooling. The context is legitimate development setup, so the risk is supply-chain exposure rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Screen and Audio Capture Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends system-level screen and audio recording. This is relevant for performance capture, but users could accidentally record private on-screen content or audio if they do not constrain the capture area.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The screen capture instruction is explicit. No upload or covert capture is present, which limits severity but still creates a clear privacy warning.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Findings Are Markdown Reference Links
low_findings.description: The ../../ sequences appear inside markdown links to repository documentation and a QA harness. I did not find evidence that the skill reads arbitrary files, writes outside the project, or instructs traversal for sensitive paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious sequences are visible only as relative links in a reference list. There is no file operation or instruction to access user secrets.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography detections map to ordinary documentation wording, such as description, design, desktop, and hides. I did not find evidence of hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose about the instrument workflow, not cryptographic code. No crypto API, algorithm selection, or security-sensitive digest operation is present.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are UI Documentation
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections refer to instrument controls and setlist preparation. I did not find evidence that the skill gathers hostnames, usernames, environment data, network state, or system inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text documents sliders and snapshot ordering only. No reconnaissance command or data collection behavior appears in the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Package Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup instructions include npm and npx commands. Users should review package scripts and dependency sources before running them from a community repository.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external commands are explicit and runnable. They are normal for web development setup but still create a dependency execution risk.

content.value_statement: Browser instruments can be hard to stage, tune, and document during a live session. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured guide for operating the Orthonotone performance interface.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to serve the project and open the browser instrument.
Maps interface controls for scenes, snapshots, timbre, rotation, MIDI, and tempo.
Guides gesture-based performance using pointer, touch, keyboard modifiers, and motion input.
Describes scene design steps for tuning lattice parameters and harmonic density.
Documents recording, QA report copying, and troubleshooting workflows.
limitations: Requires the Orthonotone repository and browser instrument files to exist locally.
Assumes a Chromium-based browser for best WebAudio timing.
Does not provide the instrument code or audio engine by itself.
External MIDI, screen recording, and desktop audio routing depend on local permissions.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Live Demo
use_cases.description: Set up the browser instrument, warm the audio graph, choose a scene, and verify the controls before a demonstration.

use_cases.title: Design a Soundscape
use_cases.description: Shape timbre, motion, harmonic density, and snapshots for an evolving performance.

use_cases.title: Capture a Reproducible Take
use_cases.description: Record visuals and sound, copy diagnostics, and attach commit context for collaborators.

prompt_templates.title: Start the Instrument
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help me launch the Orthonotone instrument locally and confirm that audio is enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Scene
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through creating a calm Orthonotone scene with balanced timbre, slow rotation, and saved snapshots.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare MIDI Control
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me connect MIDI, check tempo sync, and map the Orthonotone controls for a live set.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Recorded Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a performance capture checklist for Orthonotone, including QA diagnostics, snapshot order, and privacy-safe recording steps.

output_examples.input: Help me prepare an Orthonotone demo.
output_examples.output: A staged checklist covering local serving, browser launch, audio enablement, volume checks, scene selection, and QA verification.
A performance recipe with suggested scene choice, lattice settings, timbre adjustments, rotation balance, and snapshot naming.
A capture plan that includes screen and audio routing checks, diagnostics copying, preset notes, and commit context.

best_practices: Review npm scripts and dependencies before running commands from the community repository.
Set master volume first and test audio before adjusting performance scenes.
Capture only the instrument window or a controlled display when recording.
anti_patterns: Running setup commands without inspecting the project package scripts.
Starting a recording while unrelated private windows or audio sources are visible.
Changing many controls at once without saving snapshots or notes.

faq.question: Does this skill generate audio by itself?
faq.answer: No. It guides use of the Orthonotone browser instrument in the repository.

faq.question: Which browser is recommended?
faq.answer: The skill recommends a Chromium-based browser for reliable WebAudio timing.

faq.question: Can it help with MIDI?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains MIDI bridge setup, tempo sync checks, and controller-related troubleshooting.

faq.question: Does it require command execution?
faq.answer: The setup guidance includes npm and npx commands. Review the repository before running them.

faq.question: Does it upload recordings?
faq.answer: No evidence found. It recommends local screen and audio recording tools only.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it for QA?
faq.answer: Yes. It mentions the Playwright check workflow and copying diagnostics for reproducible reports.
